sub_mechanisms: M_1 detect singular-flat regime via gradient-magnitude < epsilon
M_2 identify null direction via Hessian-zero eigenvector
M_3 inject orthogonal perturbation along null direction
M_4 resume gradient updates once perturbation reaches non-flat region
stripped_llm_application: A plateau-escape regularizer that detects when gradient magnitude is near zero, identifies a null direction via Hessian zero-eigenspace, injects a perturbation along it, and resumes gradient descent.
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing saddle-escape (Jin Ge 2017) adds isotropic noise. Candidate injects directed perturbation along Hessian null direction motivated by Cantor-derivative-zero structure.
